heading: Designation of enterprise zones
label: 11501 Designation of enterprise zones

children.label: A
children.text: is nominated by one or more local governments and the State or States in which it is located for designation as an enterprise zone (in this section referred to as a “nominated area”); and

children.label: i
children.text: the Secretaries of Agriculture, Commerce, Labor, and the Treasury, the Director of the Office of Management and Budget, and the Administrator of the Small Business Administration; and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of an area on an Indian reservation, the Secretary of the Interior.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary of Housing and Urban Development may designate not more than 100 nominated areas as enterprise zones.

children.heading: Minimum designation in rural areas
children.label: B Minimum designation in rural areas
i
children.text: are within a local government jurisdiction or jurisdictions with a population of less than 50,000 (as determined under the most recent census data available);

children.label: ii
children.text: are outside of a metropolitan statistical area (as designated by the Director of the Office of Management and Budget); or

children.label: iii
children.text: that are determined by the Secretary, after consultation with the Secretary of Commerce, to be rural areas.

children.heading: Areas designated based solely on degree of poverty
children.label: 3 Areas designated based solely on degree of poverty

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the Secretary shall designate (i) the nominated areas with the highest average ranking with respect to the criteria set forth in subparagraphs (C) and (D) of subsection (c)(3), and the 1 criterion set forth in subparagraph (E)(i) or (E)(ii) of subsection (c)(3) that gives an area a higher ranking; and (ii) for areas described in paragraph (2)(B), the nominated areas with the highest ranking with respect to the 1 criterion set forth in subparagraph (C), (D), (E)(i), or (E)(ii) of subsection (c)(3) that gives an area a higher ranking. For purposes of the preceding sentence, an area shall be ranked within each such criterion on the basis of the amount by which the area exceeds such criterion, with the area that exceeds such criterion by the greatest amount given the highest ranking.

children.heading: Exception where inadequate course of action, etc.
children.label: B Exception where inadequate course of action, etc.
children.text: An area shall not be designated under subparagraph (A) if the Secretary determines that the course of action with respect to such area is inadequate.

children.heading: Separate application to rural and other areas
children.label: C Separate application to rural and other areas
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall be applied separately with respect to areas described in paragraph (2)(B) and to other areas.

children.label: i
children.text: the procedures for nominating an area under paragraph (1)(A);

children.label: ii
children.text: the parameters relating to the size and population characteristics of an enterprise zone; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the manner in which nominated areas will be evaluated based on the criteria specified in subsection (d).

children.heading: Time limitations
children.label: B Time limitations
children.text: The Secretary shall designate nominated areas as enterprise zones only during the 24-month period beginning on the 1st day of the 1st month following the month in which the date of the enactment of the Housing and Community Development Act of 1992 occurs.

children.label: I
children.text: to nominate such area for designation as an enterprise zone;

children.label: II
children.text: to make the State and local commitments under subsection (d); and

children.label: III
children.text: to provide assurances satisfactory to the Secretary that such commitments will be fulfilled;

children.label: ii
children.text: a nomination therefor is submitted in such a manner and in such form, and contains such information, as the Secretary shall by regulation prescribe;

children.label: iii
children.text: the Secretary determines that any information furnished is reasonably accurate; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the State and local governments certify that no portion of the area nominated is already included in an enterprise zone or in an area otherwise nominated to be an enterprise zone.

children.heading: Nomination process for Indian reservations
children.label: 5 Nomination process for Indian reservations
children.text: In the case of a nominated area on an Indian reservation, the reservation governing body (as determined by the Secretary of the Interior) shall be deemed to be both the State and local governments with respect to such area.

children.heading: Period for which designation is in effect
children.label: b Period for which designation is in effect
A
children.text: December 31 of the 24th calendar year following the calendar year in which such date occurs;

children.label: B
children.text: the termination date designated by the State and local governments as provided for in their nomination pursuant to subsection (a)(4)(C)(ii); or

children.label: C
children.text: the date the Secretary revokes such designation under paragraph (2).

children.heading: Revocation of designation
children.label: 2 Revocation of designation
children.text: The Secretary, after consultation with the officials described in subsection (a)(1)(B) and a hearing on the record involving officials of the State or local government involved, may revoke the designation of an area if the Secretary determines that the local government or the State in which it is located is not complying substantially with the State and local commitments pursuant to subsection (d).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may make a designation of any nominated area under subsection (a)(1) only if it meets the requirements of paragraphs (2) and (3).

children.label: A
children.text: the area is within the jurisdiction of the local government;

children.label: B
children.text: the boundary of the area is continuous; and

children.label: I
children.text: 4,000 if any portion of such area (other than a rural area described in subsection (a)(2)(B)(i)) is located within a metropolitan statistical area (as designated by the Director of the Office of Management and Budget) with a population of 50,000 or more; or

children.label: II
children.text: 1,000 in any other case; or

children.label: ii
children.text: is entirely within an Indian reservation (as determined by the Secretary of the Interior).

children.label: A
children.text: the area is one of pervasive poverty, unemployment, and general distress;

children.label: B
children.text: the area is located wholly within the jurisdiction of a local government that is eligible for Federal assistance under , as in effect on ;section 5318 of this titleOctober 28, 1992

children.label: C
children.text: the unemployment rate, as determined by the appropriate available data, was not less than 1.5 times the national unemployment rate for that period;

children.label: D
children.text: the poverty rate (as determined by the most recent census data available) for each populous census tract (or where not tracted, the equivalent county division as defined by the Bureau of the Census for the purpose of defining poverty areas) within the area was not less than 20 percent for the period to which such data relate; and

children.label: i
children.text: Not less than 70 percent of the households living in the area have incomes below 80 percent of the median income of households of the local government (determined in the same manner as under ).section 5318 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: The population of the area decreased by 20 percent or more between 1970 and 1980 (as determined from the most recent census available).

children.label: A
children.text: the criteria set forth in subparagraphs (A) and (B) of paragraph (3); and

children.label: B
children.text: not less than one of the criteria set forth in the other subparagraphs of paragraph (3).

children.heading: Required State and local commitments
children.label: d Required State and local commitments

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: No nominated area shall be designated as an enterprise zone unless the local government and the State in which it is located agree in writing that, during any period during which the area is an enterprise zone, such governments will follow a specified course of action designated to reduce the various burdens borne by employers or employees in such area. A course of action shall not be treated as meeting the requirements of this paragraph unless the course of action include provisions described in not less than 4 of the subparagraphs of paragraph (2).

children.label: A
children.text: a reduction of tax rates or fees applying within the enterprise zone;

children.label: B
children.text: an increase in the level of public services, or in the efficiency of the delivery of public services, within the enterprise zone;

children.label: C
children.text: actions to reduce, remove, simplify, or streamline paperwork requirements within the enterprise zone;

children.label: D
children.text: involvement in the program by public authorities or private entities, organizations, neighborhood associations, and community groups, particularly those within the nominated area, including a written commitment to provide jobs and job training for, and technical, financial, or other assistance to, employers, employees, and residents of the nominated area;

children.label: E
children.text: the giving of special preference to contractors owned and operated by members of any minority; and

children.label: F
children.text: the gift (or sale at below fair market value) of surplus land in the enterprise zone to neighborhood organizations agreeing to operate a business on the land.

children.heading: Recognition of past efforts
children.label: 3 Recognition of past efforts
children.text: In evaluating courses of action agreed to by any State or local government, the Secretary shall take into account the past efforts of such State or local government in reducing the various burdens borne by employers and employees in the area involved.

children.heading: Prohibition of assistance for business relocations
children.label: 4 Prohibition of assistance for business relocations
i
children.text: any establishment relocating from one area to another area; or

children.label: ii
children.text: any subcontractor whose purpose is to divest, or whose economic success is dependent upon divesting, any other contractor or subcontractor of any contract customarily performed by such other contractor or subcontractor.

children.label: i
children.text: finds that the establishment of the new branch, affiliate, or subsidiary will not result in an increase in unemployment in the area of original location or in any other area where the existing business entity conducts business operations; and

children.label: ii
children.text: has no reason to believe that the new branch, affiliate, or subsidiary is being established with the intention of closing down the operations of the existing business entity in the area of its original location or in any other area where the existing business entity conducts business operations.

children.heading: Government
children.label: 1 Government
children.text: If more than one government seeks to nominate an area as an enterprise zone, any reference to, or requirement of, this section shall apply to all such governments.

children.label: A
children.text: any county, city, town, township, parish, village, or other general purpose political subdivision of a State;

children.label: B
children.text: any combination of political subdivisions described in subparagraph (A) recognized by the Secretary; and

children.label: C
children.text: the District of Columbia.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 3 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development.

children.heading: State
children.label: 4 State
children.text: The term “State” includes Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, the Northern Mariana Islands, and any other possession of the United States.
